insight:
Yesterday was a solid day with your 5.1km run at a controlled effort - that 160 bpm average heart rate shows good restraint just 5 days out from Chester. Your resting HR of 45 bpm and low stress level of 21/100 indicate you're in excellent condition, while 7.4 hours of sleep hit the sweet spot for recovery.

Your HRV of 112ms sits beautifully in your baseline range, signaling your body has absorbed yesterday's training well and is ready for today's session. Combined with those strong recovery markers, you can stick to your planned workout without modifications - your system is firing on all cylinders heading into race week.

With 2,729 calories burned yesterday, you likely created a decent deficit against your 2,300 target. Today, focus on eating closer to maintenance calories to fuel your session and support recovery. Your body needs that energy now more than continued weight loss with the half marathon so close.
